name: Teaser Card

releases.author: Britz
releases.description:
v0.0.3 — community-review fixes, type-safety & test hardening

## [0.0.3] - 2026-06-29

- **Change 1 — Manifest cleanup.** Dropped the unknown `main`/`css` fields from `manifest.json` (Obsidian loads `main.js`/`styles.css` by convention).
- **Change 2 — Detect UI language via `getLanguage()`.** The settings UI now reads Obsidian's `getLanguage()` instead of `localStorage`, removing the flagged direct storage access (T6).
- **Change 3 — Popout-window compatibility.** The global `--teaser-card-width` is set on `activeDocument.body` instead of `document.body`.
- **Change 4 — Type-safety hardening.** Removed the unsafe `any` assignments in settings load and the parser, and explicitly voided the floating promises in the renderer and resolver.
- **Change 5 — Replace js-yaml test dependency.** The unit-test `obsidian` stub now uses the maintained `yaml` package instead of `js-yaml` (test-only; the shipped bundle is unchanged).

releases.author: Britz
releases.description:
v0.0.2 — wrapping layout, click & width settings, localized settings UI

## [0.0.2] - 2026-06-28

- **Decision 1 — Wrapping layout replaces the fixed grid.** `.teaser-card-grid` is now `flex-wrap` (D7): cards keep their width and wrap to the next row, collapsing to one column when narrower than a card.
- **Feature 1 — Live-preview click-action setting.** A plugin setting (F9) toggles whether clicking a card in live preview opens its link (default) or jumps the cursor to the block's source; reading view always opens the link.
- **Feature 2 — Per-card CSS class.** An optional `class` field adds author CSS class(es) to the card element alongside the structural classes, so snippets can target specific cards (T5).
- **Feature 3 — Per-card width.** An optional `width` field sets the card width via the `--teaser-card-width` contract property (bare number → `px`), controlling how many cards fit per row.
- **Feature 4 — Click an error to open its source.** Clicking the in-place error notice (F8) opens the note's source so the block can be fixed: reading view switches to the editor, live preview jumps the cursor to the fault line (YAML syntax errors carry it) or the block start.
- **Feature 5 — Syntax reference in settings.** The settings tab now shows a read-only reference for the `teaser` block syntax (single/multiple cards and the per-card fields) alongside the F9 toggle, under a header with a community-plugins store link and a book icon — authoring help, not configuration (T3).
- **Feature 6 — Localized settings UI.** All settings-tab strings are localized (English/German) following Obsidian's UI language (T6).
